description:
Goal: make compile-time scope a single compiler2 snapshot derived from namespace plus current module/function/caller context, and project `__ENV__`, `__MODULE__`, and `__CALLER__` as Fz-shaped data only at macro/source boundaries.

Signal:
- there is no second mutable `CompileEnv` authority or fake env-only namespace binding layer
- `__MODULE__` is derived from the current module id/name already tracked by compiler2
- current function/definition context is derived from the function/module being processed, not stored in parallel state
- `__CALLER__` and the needed `__ENV__` fields are projected from the current scope snapshot plus quoted-source lexical/span metadata
- tests prove scope snapshots thread through module/function boundaries without copying whole scopes or polluting ordinary name resolution
- quoted identifiers can carry the definition/caller lexical context required by the hygiene pressure test

Change:
- tighten compiler2's scope model around one authoritative snapshot: namespace head plus the module/function/source metadata needed for projection
- derive `__MODULE__`, current function context, and the required `__CALLER__` / `__ENV__` Fz values from that snapshot instead of introducing a parallel env store
- thread the snapshot through module/function/macro/source-production paths in a way that keeps ordinary resolution and compiler-variable projection separate
- keep caller-vs-definition lexical/span context in quoted-source metadata, not in fake namespace bindings
- leave `require` / imported-macro availability and provider waits to `.11.7.6`; this ticket is about projection and authority shape, not macro-provider dependency scanning

Why:
- namespace is already the durable resolution authority, and module/function identity already exists in compiler2 state
- adding a second env store for data that is derivable would duplicate authority and make the design sloppier, not simpler
- the only extra state that must survive macro boundaries is caller/definition/source metadata, which belongs in the quoted-source layer
title: Compiler2 scope snapshot: project __ENV__/__MODULE__/__CALLER__ without a second env store
